title: ampelocissus obtusata
intro: Ampelocissus obtusata, also known as the Indian grapevine, is a type of climbing plant native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ampelocissus obtusata include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating various skin conditions, as well as providing antioxidant protection against cell damage. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and fruits are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
